---
title: "Phase C — Interactive simulator"
subtitle: "Live Monte Carlo on parameters of your choosing"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .phase-c-derived { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(180px, 1fr)); gap: 0.75rem; margin: 1rem 0; }
  .phase-c-derived .card { border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 6px; padding: 0.6rem 0.75rem; background: #fff; }
  .phase-c-derived .card .label { font-size: 0.8rem; color: #555; }
  .phase-c-derived .card .value { font-family: ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 1.1rem; }

  /* Section layout: controls in a left rail that's sticky across the whole
     section; body content aligns with Quarto's body column. The .pc-section
     uses .column-screen-inset-left, whose right edge coincides with the
     body column's right edge, so placing the content column at the grid's
     right edge with the body column's width reproduces the body column
     exactly. The sticky sidebar sits in the left whitespace. */
  .pc-section {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) 320px 1.5rem minmax(0, 850px);
    align-items: start;
  }
  .pc-section > * { grid-column: 4; min-width: 0; }
  .pc-section > .pc-controls {
    grid-column: 2;
    grid-row: 1 / -1;
    position: sticky; top: 0.75rem;
    align-self: start;
    max-height: calc(100vh - 1.5rem);
    overflow-y: auto;
  }
  .pc-controls {
    padding: 0.75rem 1rem 1rem;
    border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; background: #fafafa;
    font-size: 0.85rem;
  }
  .pc-controls h3 { font-size: 0.9rem; margin: 0.9rem 0 0.4rem; color: #333; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; border-bottom: 1px solid #e0e0e0; padding-bottom: 0.2rem; }
  .pc-controls p { font-size: 0.82rem; color: #555; margin: 0.25rem 0 0.6rem; }
  /* Observable Inputs truncate long labels by default — let them wrap. */
  .pc-controls form[class*="oi-"] > label {
    width: auto !important;
    min-width: 90px; max-width: 130px;
    white-space: normal !important;
    line-height: 1.2;
  }
  /* Narrow screens: drop the grid, unstick the controls. */
  @media (max-width: 1100px) {
    .pc-section { display: block; }
    .pc-section > .pc-controls { position: static; max-height: none; }
  }

  .pc-readout { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 0.3rem 0.6rem; margin: 0.4rem 0 0.2rem; font-size: 0.8rem; }
  .pc-readout .k { color: #555; }
  .pc-readout .v { font-family: ui-monospace, monospace; text-align: right; }
</style>
```

## Starting Phase C

[Phase B](report.qmd) cross-checked the closed-form §1–§5 quantities (see
the [Phase A note](phase-a.qmd)) against Monte Carlo and exposed a
**pre-baked** sweep over $(\alpha, \mu, \sigma)$ through the Observable
notebook. Phase C extends the modelling menu — jump diffusion, regime
switching, Poisson retirements, historical calibration, dynamic hedging —
and the first piece of scaffolding it needs is **a way to run the
simulator live on parameters of our choosing**, not just a pre-computed grid.

This page is that scaffolding. Every slider below triggers a fresh Monte
Carlo in the browser (no server round-trip, no artifact regeneration). The
baseline price process is the same GBM used in [Phase B](report.qmd);
subsequent Phase C iterations will swap in the richer dynamics behind the
same UI.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## What changes vs. [Phase B](report.qmd)

The pre-purchase hedge is now specified by the **raw inventory state** —
a number of kVCM tokens and their USD cost basis — instead of the ratio
$\alpha$. This removes the Phase B assumption that the tokens were
bought today at $S_0$: you can seed the simulation with an inventory
that was acquired earlier at a different price. The Phase B $(\alpha =
k / (N \cdot P),\; \text{basis} = \alpha \cdot N \cdot P \cdot S_0)$
parametrisation is a special case of the new one.
:::

:::: {.pc-section .column-screen-inset-left}

::: {.pc-controls}
### Price process

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof pi0 = Inputs.range([0, 500], { step: 1, value: 125, label: "π₀ (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof S0 = Inputs.range([0.005, 1], { step: 0.005, value: 0.07, label: "S₀ (USD / kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof mu = Inputs.range([-0.5, 0.5], { step: 0.005, value: 0.05, label: "drift μ (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigma2 = Inputs.range([0, 2.5], { step: 0.005, value: 0.25, label: "variance σ² (annualised)" })
```

### Demand & inventory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaDay = Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, value: 1, label: "retirements (tonnes / day)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof kPre = Inputs.range([0, 2000000], { step: 1000, value: 300000, label: "initial inventory (kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof cBasis = Inputs.range([0, 1000000], { step: 100, value: 21000, label: "inventory basis (USD, total)" })
```

### Contract

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Tdays = Inputs.range([30, 1095], { step: 5, value: 365, label: "horizon T (days)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Q = Inputs.range([0, 1000], { step: 0.5, value: 180, label: "fixed quote Q (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fee = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee rate f" })
```

### Monte Carlo

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof nPaths = Inputs.select([2000, 5000, 10000, 20000, 40000], { value: 5000, label: "paths" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof nSteps = Inputs.select([50, 100, 200, 365], { value: 100, label: "steps / path" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof seed = Inputs.range([1, 9999], { step: 1, value: 42, label: "PRNG seed" })
```

### Live readout

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="pc-readout">
  <div class="k">P = π₀ / S₀</div><div class="v">${derived.P.toFixed(3)}</div>
  <div class="k">σ</div><div class="v">${derived.sigma.toFixed(3)}</div>
  <div class="k">T (yr)</div><div class="v">${derived.T.toFixed(2)}</div>
  <div class="k">N (tonnes)</div><div class="v">${derived.N.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div>
  <div class="k">coverage</div><div class="v">${(derived.coverageFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div>
  <div class="k">Q* (USD/t)</div><div class="v">${derived.QStar.toFixed(2)}</div>
</div>`
```

:::

Drift $\mu$ and variance $\sigma^2$ are annualised. The retirement rate
$\lambda$ is specified per calendar day; internally we use a year as the
time unit, so $\lambda_{\mathrm{yr}} = 365 \cdot \lambda_{\mathrm{day}}$.
Defaults are set so that $Q$ tracks the Phase B fee-equivalent quote at
$t = 0$; override to study off-market pricing.

## Derived quantities

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
derived = {
  const sigma = Math.sqrt(sigma2);
  const P = pi0 / S0;
  const T = Tdays / 365;
  const lambda = lambdaDay * 365;
  const N = lambda * T;
  const tokensNeeded = N * P;
  const coverageDays = (lambda > 0 && P > 0) ? kPre / (lambda * P) * 365 : Infinity;
  const coverageFrac = tokensNeeded > 0 ? Math.min(1, kPre / tokensNeeded) : 0;
  const impliedCost = kPre > 0 ? cBasis / kPre : 0;
  const QStarRatio = expm1OverX(mu * T);
  const QStar = (1 + fee) * P * S0 * QStarRatio;
  return { sigma, P, T, lambda, N, tokensNeeded, coverageDays, coverageFrac, impliedCost, QStar };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="phase-c-derived">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">σ (volatility)</div><div class="value">${derived.sigma.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">P = π₀ / S₀ (kVCM / tonne)</div><div class="value">${derived.P.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">T (years)</div><div class="value">${derived.T.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">N = λ·T (tonnes over horizon)</div><div class="value">${derived.N.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">tokens needed N·P</div><div class="value">${derived.tokensNeeded.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">coverage from inventory</div><div class="value">${(derived.coverageFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">inventory runs out at (days)</div><div class="value">${isFinite(derived.coverageDays) ? Math.min(derived.coverageDays, Tdays).toFixed(1) : "∞"}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">implied basis per token</div><div class="value">${kPre > 0 ? "$" + derived.impliedCost.toFixed(3) : "—"}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">break-even Q* (USD / tonne)</div><div class="value">${derived.QStar.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
</div>`
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## Interpreting "coverage"

If the pre-purchased inventory covers 100% of the horizon's token
demand, the book is fully matched (§3a-style). Below 100%, the operator
runs out of inventory part-way through the horizon and has to buy the
remainder back-to-back at spot — that is where the left-tail risk
appears. Above 100% (over-hedged) the excess tokens are marked-to-market
at $S_T$ at the horizon.
:::

## Core simulator

All cells below are pure JavaScript running in the browser — no server, no
pre-computed JSON. The simulator is a direct port of `src/rng.ts`,
`src/gbm.ts`, `src/moments.ts`, and `src/risk.ts`. Keeping it inline lets
readers audit every calculation in one place.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function makeRng(seedVal) {
  let state = seedVal >>> 0;
  let cached = null;
  const uniform = () => {
    state = (state + 0x6d2b79f5) >>> 0;
    let t = state;
    t = Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 15), t | 1);
    t ^= t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), t | 61);
    const u = ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;
    return u === 0 ? 1 / 4294967296 : u;
  };
  const normal = () => {
    if (cached !== null) {
      const z = cached;
      cached = null;
      return z;
    }
    const u1 = uniform();
    const u2 = uniform();
    const r = Math.sqrt(-2 * Math.log(u1));
    const th = 2 * Math.PI * u2;
    cached = r * Math.sin(th);
    return r * Math.cos(th);
  };
  return { uniform, normal };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function expm1OverX(x) {
  if (x === 0) return 1;
  if (Math.abs(x) < 1e-8) return 1 + x / 2 + (x * x) / 6;
  return Math.expm1(x) / x;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function quantileSorted(sorted, q) {
  const n = sorted.length;
  if (n === 0) return NaN;
  if (n === 1) return sorted[0];
  const pos = q * (n - 1);
  const lo = Math.floor(pos);
  const hi = Math.ceil(pos);
  if (lo === hi) return sorted[lo];
  const frac = pos - lo;
  return sorted[lo] * (1 - frac) + sorted[hi] * frac;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function tailMean(sortedAscending, q) {
  const n = sortedAscending.length;
  const k = Math.max(1, Math.ceil((1 - q) * n));
  let s = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < k; i++) s += sortedAscending[i];
  return s / k;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function simulate(inputs) {
  const { S0, mu, sigma, P, lambda, T, Q, fee, kPre, cBasis, nPaths, nSteps, seed } = inputs;
  const rng = makeRng(seed);
  const dt = T / nSteps;
  const drift = (mu - 0.5 * sigma * sigma) * dt;
  const diffusion = sigma * Math.sqrt(dt);
  const N = lambda * T;
  // Fraction of horizon over which inventory lasts, clamped to [0, 1].
  // Retirements are a constant flow λ, so `kPre` tokens cover
  // τ = kPre / (λ P) years of demand.
  const tauFrac = (lambda > 0 && P > 0)
    ? Math.min(1, kPre / (lambda * P * T))
    : 1;
  const tokensUsedInternal = Math.min(kPre, lambda * T * P);
  const tokensLeftover = Math.max(0, kPre - lambda * T * P);
  // When inventory covers the whole horizon, the tail integral range [τT, T]
  // has zero length; park tailStartStep beyond the loop so no node ever
  // contributes. (Math.ceil(1 · nSteps) = nSteps would otherwise add
  // 0.5·S_T·dt from the single collapsed endpoint.)
  // Snap tauFrac onto the integration grid and treat "only the endpoint
  // falls in [τT, T]" as an empty range — otherwise the trapezoid rule
  // would contribute 0.5·S_T·dt for a zero-length interval.
  const tailStartRaw = Math.ceil(tauFrac * nSteps);
  const tailStartStep = tailStartRaw >= nSteps ? nSteps + 1 : tailStartRaw;

  const feeSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const principalSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const b2bSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const ITSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const terminalS = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const keepPaths = Math.min(20, nPaths);
  const sampledPaths = [];

  for (let i = 0; i < nPaths; i++) {
    // Trapezoid accumulators: full-horizon I_T and uncovered-tail integral
    // ∫_{τ T}^{T} S_t dt. We stitch the tail integral out of the same
    // trajectory so it uses the same realisations as I_T.
    let S = S0;
    let IT = 0;
    let tailInt = 0;
    const path = (i < keepPaths) ? new Float64Array(nSteps + 1) : null;
    if (path) path[0] = S;

    // Trapezoid rule: weight endpoints by 1/2.
    IT += 0.5 * S;
    if (tailStartStep === 0) tailInt += 0.5 * S;

    for (let k = 1; k <= nSteps; k++) {
      const z = rng.normal();
      S = S * Math.exp(drift + diffusion * z);
      if (path) path[k] = S;
      const w = (k === nSteps) ? 0.5 : 1;
      IT += w * S;
      if (k >= tailStartStep) {
        const wt = (k === tailStartStep || k === nSteps) ? 0.5 : 1;
        tailInt += wt * S;
      }
    }
    IT *= dt;
    tailInt *= dt;
    terminalS[i] = S;
    ITSamples[i] = IT;

    // Fee book: R_fee = f · P · λ · I_T.
    feeSamples[i] = fee * P * lambda * IT;

    // Back-to-back principal book (no pre-purchase): Π = Q·N − P·λ·I_T.
    b2bSamples[i] = Q * N - P * lambda * IT;

    // Custom principal with initial inventory:
    //   Π = Q·N − C_basis − P·λ·∫_{τT}^{T} S_t dt + leftover · S_T.
    // The cost basis is a sunk cost at t=0; uncovered retirements after
    // inventory exhaustion are bought at spot; excess tokens mark-to-market
    // at horizon.
    principalSamples[i] =
      Q * N - cBasis - P * lambda * tailInt + tokensLeftover * S;

    if (path) sampledPaths.push(path);
  }

  return {
    feeSamples,
    principalSamples,
    b2bSamples,
    ITSamples,
    terminalS,
    sampledPaths,
    tauFrac,
    tokensUsedInternal,
    tokensLeftover,
    N,
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function summarise(samples) {
  const n = samples.length;
  let sum = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) sum += samples[i];
  const mean = sum / n;
  let sse = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
    const d = samples[i] - mean;
    sse += d * d;
  }
  const variance = n > 1 ? sse / (n - 1) : 0;
  const sd = Math.sqrt(variance);
  const stderr = sd / Math.sqrt(n);
  const sorted = Float64Array.from(samples); sorted.sort();
  const var95 = -quantileSorted(sorted, 0.05);
  const var99 = -quantileSorted(sorted, 0.01);
  const cvar95 = -tailMean(sorted, 0.95);
  const cvar99 = -tailMean(sorted, 0.99);
  let losses = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) if (samples[i] < 0) losses++;
  const probLoss = losses / n;
  const sharpe = sd > 0 ? mean / sd : null;
  return { mean, sd, stderr, ci95: 1.96 * stderr, var95, var99, cvar95, cvar99, probLoss, sharpe };
}
```

## Monte Carlo run

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
run = {
  const inputs = {
    S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P: derived.P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee, kPre, cBasis,
    nPaths, nSteps, seed,
  };
  const t0 = performance.now();
  const raw = simulate(inputs);
  const elapsedMs = performance.now() - t0;
  return {
    inputs,
    ...raw,
    fee: summarise(raw.feeSamples),
    principal: summarise(raw.principalSamples),
    b2b: summarise(raw.b2bSamples),
    elapsedMs,
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="phase-c-derived">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">paths simulated</div><div class="value">${nPaths.toLocaleString()}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">wall time</div><div class="value">${run.elapsedMs.toFixed(1)} ms</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">coverage this run</div><div class="value">${(run.tauFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">leftover tokens at T</div><div class="value">${run.tokensLeftover.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
</div>`
```

### Summary statistics

Three books are simulated on each draw:

- **Fee** — $R_{\mathrm{fee}} = f \cdot P \cdot \lambda \cdot I_T$, revenue only.
- **Principal (back-to-back)** — $\Pi_{\mathrm{b2b}} = Q \cdot N - P \cdot \lambda \cdot I_T$. No pre-purchase, for reference.
- **Principal (custom inventory)** — $\Pi_{\mathrm{cust}} = Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}} - P \cdot \lambda \int_{\tau T}^{T} S_t\,dt + k_{\mathrm{left}} \cdot S_T$, the active slider model.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
summaryRows = [
  { book: "fee", ...run.fee },
  { book: "principal (custom)", ...run.principal },
  { book: "principal (back-to-back)", ...run.b2b },
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(summaryRows, {
  columns: ["book","mean","sd","ci95","var95","var99","cvar95","cvar99","probLoss","sharpe"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    mean: "E[Π] (mc)",
    sd: "SD",
    ci95: "±CI95",
    var95: "VaR95",
    var99: "VaR99",
    cvar95: "CVaR95",
    cvar99: "CVaR99",
    probLoss: "P[Π<0]",
    sharpe: "Sharpe",
  },
  format: {
    mean: x => x.toFixed(2),
    sd: x => x.toFixed(2),
    ci95: x => "±" + x.toFixed(2),
    var95: x => x.toFixed(2),
    var99: x => x.toFixed(2),
    cvar95: x => x.toFixed(2),
    cvar99: x => x.toFixed(2),
    probLoss: x => x.toFixed(3),
    sharpe: x => x === null ? "—" : x.toFixed(3),
  }
})
```

### P&L distributions

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pnlRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const push = (label, arr) => {
    for (let i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) rows.push({ book: label, pnl: arr[i] });
  };
  // Subsample for plotting responsiveness.
  const stride = Math.max(1, Math.floor(run.feeSamples.length / 2500));
  for (let i = 0; i < run.feeSamples.length; i += stride) {
    rows.push({ book: "fee", pnl: run.feeSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "principal (custom)", pnl: run.principalSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "principal (back-to-back)", pnl: run.b2bSamples[i] });
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 900,
  height: 440,
  marginLeft: 80,
  marginRight: 30,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "terminal P&L (USD)", tickFormat: "~s" },
  y: { label: "empirical density" },
  fy: { label: "book" },
  color: { legend: true, domain: ["fee","principal (custom)","principal (back-to-back)"] },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.ruleX([0], { stroke: "#888", strokeDasharray: "2 4" }),
    Plot.rectY(pnlRecords, Plot.binX({ y: "proportion" }, { x: "pnl", fill: "book", fy: "book", thresholds: 50 })),
    Plot.ruleX([
      { book: "fee", x: run.fee.mean },
      { book: "principal (custom)", x: run.principal.mean },
      { book: "principal (back-to-back)", x: run.b2b.mean },
    ], { x: "x", fy: "book", stroke: "black", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ]
})
```

_Solid black: MC-estimated $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$. Dashed grey: $\Pi = 0$. The
custom-principal panel collapses toward a spike as coverage approaches
100% — that's the §3a matched book limit._

### Sample price paths and inventory decay

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pathRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const paths = run.sampledPaths;
  const nS = run.inputs.nSteps;
  for (let idx = 0; idx < paths.length; idx++) {
    const path = paths[idx];
    for (let k = 0; k <= nS; k++) {
      rows.push({
        tDays: (k / nS) * Tdays,
        S: path[k],
        id: idx,
      });
    }
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 900,
  height: 340,
  marginLeft: 70,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "t (days)", domain: [0, Tdays] },
  y: { label: "kVCM spot S_t (USD)", zero: true, tickFormat: "~s" },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.line(pathRecords, { x: "tDays", y: "S", z: "id", stroke: "#888", strokeOpacity: 0.35 }),
    Plot.line(pathRecords.filter(r => r.id === 0), { x: "tDays", y: "S", stroke: "#c94", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    Plot.ruleX([run.tauFrac * Tdays], { stroke: "#2a7", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ]
})
```

_Grey: sampled GBM trajectories of the kVCM spot. Orange: first path
highlighted. Green dashed rule: time at which the pre-purchased inventory
is exhausted — to the right of this line, retirements are sourced at
spot._

::::

## Custom price curve scenario

The Monte Carlo above averages over a GBM's random futures. Sometimes you
want the opposite: fix **one** specific price trajectory and see what the
books do under it. Draw (or click-and-drag) a curve below — a crash, a
spike, a choppy flat — and the three book P&Ls are recomputed
deterministically from that trajectory in real time, using the same
parameters as the sliders in the left pane.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## How the curve is used

The drawn values $\tilde S_0, \tilde S_1, \ldots, \tilde S_n$ define the
base trajectory. If the noise variance $\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}}$ is
positive, each step is multiplied by an accumulated log-normal shock
$\exp\!\big(\sigma_{\mathrm{noise}}\sqrt{dt}\,Z_k - \tfrac12
\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}}\,dt\big)$ (reusing the Phase C MC seed so runs
are reproducible). Integrals on that realised path are computed by the
trapezoid rule ($I_T = \int_0^T S_t\,dt$, tail integral over $[\tau T,
T]$), and $S_T$ is read off the last point. All book formulas are
identical to the MC ones; only the price path differs.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
  .pc-curve-box { border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; background: #fafafa; padding: 0.75rem 1rem 1rem; margin: 0.75rem 0; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.4rem; margin: 0 0 0.6rem; align-items: center; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar button { padding: 0.3rem 0.7rem; border: 1px solid #888; background: #fff; color: #111; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 500; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar button:hover { background: #eef; border-color: #555; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar .hint { font-size: 0.82rem; color: #666; margin-left: auto; }
  .pc-curve-canvas { display: block; max-width: 100%; height: auto; touch-action: none; cursor: crosshair; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 6px; }
</style>
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
historicalPrices = {
  // Fetched at build time by `npm run fetch:prices` (Alchemy Prices API).
  // Missing or empty file ⇒ the "Historical" preset below is inert.
  try {
    const r = await fetch("data/kvcm-historical.json");
    if (!r.ok) return { data: [] };
    return await r.json();
  } catch (e) {
    return { data: [] };
  }
}
```

:::: {.pc-section .column-screen-inset-left}

::: {.pc-controls}
All sliders below mirror the ones in the previous section — moving either
copy updates both, so the drawn-curve scenario and the Monte Carlo above
stay on the same parameter set.

### Price process

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curvePi0 = Inputs.bind(Inputs.range([0, 500], { step: 1, label: "π₀ (USD / tonne)" }), viewof pi0)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveS0 = Inputs.bind(Inputs.range([0.005, 1], { step: 0.005, label: "S₀ (USD / kVCM)" }), viewof S0)
```

### Demand & inventory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveLambdaDay = Inputs.bind(Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, label: "retirements (tonnes / day)" }), viewof lambdaDay)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveKPre = Inputs.bind(Inputs.range([0, 2000000], { step: 1000, label: "initial inventory (kVCM)" }), viewof kPre)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveCBasis = Inputs.bind(Inputs.range([0, 1000000], { step: 100, label: "inventory basis (USD, total)" }), viewof cBasis)
```

### Contract

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveTdays = Inputs.bind(Inputs.range([30, 1095], { step: 5, label: "horizon T (days)" }), viewof Tdays)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveQ = Inputs.bind(Inputs.range([0, 1000], { step: 0.5, label: "fixed quote Q (USD / tonne)" }), viewof Q)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveFee = Inputs.bind(Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, label: "fee rate f" }), viewof fee)
```

### Curve settings

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveYMax = Inputs.range([0.01, 3], { step: 0.005, value: 0.2, label: "canvas y-max (USD / kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveVar = Inputs.range([0, 2.5], { step: 0.005, value: 0, label: "noise variance σ² (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveSeed = Inputs.bind(Inputs.range([1, 9999], { step: 1, label: "PRNG seed" }), viewof seed)
```

:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof customCurve = {
  const nPoints = 201;
  const width = 820;
  const height = 320;
  const margin = { top: 10, right: 12, bottom: 32, left: 58 };
  const plotW = width - margin.left - margin.right;
  const plotH = height - margin.top - margin.bottom;
  const yMax = curveYMax;
  const S0Val = S0;
  const TDaysVal = Tdays;
  const history = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];

  const values = new Float64Array(nPoints).fill(S0Val);

  const container = document.createElement("div");
  container.className = "pc-curve-box";

  const toolbar = document.createElement("div");
  toolbar.className = "pc-curve-toolbar";
  const hint = document.createElement("span");
  hint.className = "hint";
  hint.textContent = "Click and drag on the canvas to draw a price path.";

  const canvas = document.createElement("canvas");
  canvas.className = "pc-curve-canvas";
  canvas.width = width;
  canvas.height = height;
  const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");

  const indexToX = (i) => margin.left + (i / (nPoints - 1)) * plotW;
  const priceToY = (p) => margin.top + (1 - p / yMax) * plotH;
  const xToIndex = (x) =>
    Math.max(0, Math.min(nPoints - 1, Math.round(((x - margin.left) / plotW) * (nPoints - 1))));
  const yToPrice = (y) =>
    Math.max(0, Math.min(yMax, yMax - ((y - margin.top) / plotH) * yMax));

  function redraw() {
    ctx.clearRect(0, 0, width, height);
    ctx.strokeStyle = "#e5e5e5";
    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
    ctx.beginPath();
    for (let g = 1; g < 5; g++) {
      const y = margin.top + (g * plotH) / 5;
      ctx.moveTo(margin.left, y);
      ctx.lineTo(margin.left + plotW, y);
    }
    for (let g = 1; g < 5; g++) {
      const x = margin.left + (g * plotW) / 5;
      ctx.moveTo(x, margin.top);
      ctx.lineTo(x, margin.top + plotH);
    }
    ctx.stroke();

    ctx.strokeStyle = "#333";
    ctx.strokeRect(margin.left, margin.top, plotW, plotH);

    ctx.fillStyle = "#333";
    ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
    ctx.textAlign = "right";
    ctx.textBaseline = "middle";
    for (let g = 0; g <= 5; g++) {
      const priceVal = yMax * (1 - g / 5);
      const y = margin.top + (g * plotH) / 5;
      ctx.fillText(priceVal.toFixed(3), margin.left - 6, y);
    }
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.textBaseline = "top";
    for (let g = 0; g <= 5; g++) {
      const tDay = (TDaysVal * g) / 5;
      const x = margin.left + (g * plotW) / 5;
      ctx.fillText(tDay.toFixed(0), x, margin.top + plotH + 6);
    }

    ctx.font = "12px sans-serif";
    ctx.save();
    ctx.translate(14, margin.top + plotH / 2);
    ctx.rotate(-Math.PI / 2);
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.fillText("S_t (USD / kVCM)", 0, 0);
    ctx.restore();
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.fillText("t (days)", margin.left + plotW / 2, height - 2);

    if (S0Val > 0 && S0Val <= yMax) {
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#2a7";
      ctx.setLineDash([4, 3]);
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(margin.left, priceToY(S0Val));
      ctx.lineTo(margin.left + plotW, priceToY(S0Val));
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.setLineDash([]);
      ctx.fillStyle = "#2a7";
      ctx.textAlign = "left";
      ctx.textBaseline = "bottom";
      ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
      ctx.fillText("S₀", margin.left + 4, priceToY(S0Val) - 2);
    }

    ctx.strokeStyle = "#c94";
    ctx.lineWidth = 2;
    ctx.lineJoin = "round";
    ctx.beginPath();
    ctx.moveTo(indexToX(0), priceToY(values[0]));
    for (let i = 1; i < nPoints; i++) ctx.lineTo(indexToX(i), priceToY(values[i]));
    ctx.stroke();
  }

  function emit() {
    container.value = { values: Float64Array.from(values), nPoints };
    container.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
  }

  let lastIdx = null;
  let drawing = false;

  function apply(ev) {
    const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x = ((ev.clientX - rect.left) * canvas.width) / rect.width;
    const y = ((ev.clientY - rect.top) * canvas.height) / rect.height;
    const idx = xToIndex(x);
    const price = yToPrice(y);
    if (lastIdx === null || lastIdx === idx) {
      values[idx] = price;
    } else {
      const lo = Math.min(idx, lastIdx);
      const hi = Math.max(idx, lastIdx);
      const loP = lastIdx <= idx ? values[lastIdx] : price;
      const hiP = lastIdx <= idx ? price : values[lastIdx];
      for (let k = lo; k <= hi; k++) {
        const frac = (k - lo) / (hi - lo);
        values[k] = loP * (1 - frac) + hiP * frac;
      }
    }
    lastIdx = idx;
  }

  canvas.addEventListener("pointerdown", (ev) => {
    ev.preventDefault();
    drawing = true;
    lastIdx = null;
    canvas.setPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId);
    apply(ev);
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  canvas.addEventListener("pointermove", (ev) => {
    if (!drawing) return;
    ev.preventDefault();
    apply(ev);
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  const endStroke = (ev) => {
    drawing = false;
    lastIdx = null;
    if (canvas.hasPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId)) canvas.releasePointerCapture(ev.pointerId);
  };
  canvas.addEventListener("pointerup", endStroke);
  canvas.addEventListener("pointercancel", endStroke);

  function setPreset(fn) {
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) values[i] = fn(i / (nPoints - 1));
    redraw();
    emit();
  }
  function addButton(label, fn) {
    const b = document.createElement("button");
    b.type = "button";
    b.textContent = label;
    b.addEventListener("click", fn);
    toolbar.appendChild(b);
  }
  addButton("Flat @ S₀", () => setPreset(() => S0Val));
  addButton("Linear +50%", () => setPreset((u) => S0Val * (1 + 0.5 * u)));
  addButton("Linear −50%", () => setPreset((u) => S0Val * (1 - 0.5 * u)));
  addButton("Crash @ ½T", () =>
    setPreset((u) => (u < 0.5 ? S0Val : S0Val * Math.max(0.05, 1 - 1.6 * (u - 0.5))))
  );
  addButton("Spike @ ⅓T", () => {
    const peak = S0Val * 2.5;
    setPreset((u) => {
      const d = u - 1 / 3;
      return S0Val + (peak - S0Val) * Math.exp(-80 * d * d);
    });
  });
  addButton("Random walk", () => {
    let s = S0Val;
    const vals = [];
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      s = Math.max(0, s * (1 + 0.02 * (Math.random() - 0.5)));
      vals.push(s);
    }
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) values[i] = vals[i];
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  const historicalBtn = document.createElement("button");
  historicalBtn.type = "button";
  historicalBtn.textContent = history.length > 0
    ? `Historical (${history.length}d, ${history[0].timestamp.slice(0, 10)}→${history[history.length - 1].timestamp.slice(0, 10)})`
    : "Historical (unavailable)";
  historicalBtn.disabled = history.length < 2;
  historicalBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    // Map the daily historical series onto the nPoints grid by linear
    // interpolation in index space. The drawn curve therefore spans the
    // same number of fetched days end-to-end, independent of Tdays — the
    // sliders treat it as a T-day trajectory regardless.
    const m = history.length;
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      const t = (i / (nPoints - 1)) * (m - 1);
      const lo = Math.floor(t);
      const hi = Math.min(m - 1, lo + 1);
      const frac = t - lo;
      values[i] = history[lo].value * (1 - frac) + history[hi].value * frac;
    }
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  toolbar.appendChild(historicalBtn);
  toolbar.appendChild(hint);

  container.appendChild(toolbar);
  container.appendChild(canvas);
  container.value = { values: Float64Array.from(values), nPoints };

  redraw();
  return container;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customScenario = {
  const { values: base, nPoints } = customCurve;
  const T = derived.T;
  const P = derived.P;
  const lam = derived.lambda;
  const N = derived.N;
  const dt = T / (nPoints - 1);
  const sigmaN = Math.sqrt(curveVar);

  // Realised path = drawn curve * accumulated log-normal noise.
  // Noise is zero-drift (−½σ² dt correction) so the mean is the drawn curve.
  const realised = new Float64Array(nPoints);
  if (sigmaN === 0) {
    for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) realised[k] = base[k];
  } else {
    const rng = makeRng(seed);
    let logNoise = 0;
    realised[0] = base[0];
    for (let k = 1; k < nPoints; k++) {
      const z = rng.normal();
      logNoise += sigmaN * Math.sqrt(dt) * z - 0.5 * sigmaN * sigmaN * dt;
      realised[k] = base[k] * Math.exp(logNoise);
    }
  }

  let IT = 0;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const w = (k === 0 || k === nPoints - 1) ? 0.5 : 1;
    IT += w * realised[k];
  }
  IT *= dt;

  const tauFrac = (lam > 0 && P > 0)
    ? Math.min(1, kPre / (lam * P * T))
    : 1;
  const tailStartRaw = Math.ceil(tauFrac * (nPoints - 1));
  const tailStartIdx = tailStartRaw >= nPoints - 1 ? nPoints : tailStartRaw;
  let tailInt = 0;
  for (let k = tailStartIdx; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const w = (k === tailStartIdx || k === nPoints - 1) ? 0.5 : 1;
    tailInt += w * realised[k];
  }
  tailInt *= dt;

  const ST = realised[nPoints - 1];
  const tokensLeftover = Math.max(0, kPre - lam * T * P);
  const feeRev = fee * P * lam * IT;
  const b2b = Q * N - P * lam * IT;
  const cust = Q * N - cBasis - P * lam * tailInt + tokensLeftover * ST;

  let maxS = -Infinity, minS = Infinity;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    if (realised[k] > maxS) maxS = realised[k];
    if (realised[k] < minS) minS = realised[k];
  }
  const meanS = IT / T;

  return { base, realised, nPoints, IT, tailInt, ST, tauFrac, tokensLeftover, feeRev, b2b, cust, maxS, minS, meanS };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customPathRecords = {
  const { base, realised, nPoints } = customScenario;
  const rows = [];
  const noisy = curveVar > 0;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const tDays = (k / (nPoints - 1)) * Tdays;
    rows.push({ t: tDays, S: base[k], kind: "drawn (base)" });
    if (noisy) rows.push({ t: tDays, S: realised[k], kind: "realised (+ noise)" });
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 820,
  height: 200,
  marginLeft: 58,
  marginBottom: 32,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "t (days)", domain: [0, Tdays] },
  y: { label: "S_t (USD / kVCM)", zero: true, tickFormat: "~g" },
  color: {
    legend: true,
    domain: ["drawn (base)", "realised (+ noise)"],
    range: ["#c94", "#4c78a8"],
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.line(customPathRecords, { x: "t", y: "S", stroke: "kind", strokeOpacity: d => d.kind === "drawn (base)" ? 1 : 0.7 }),
  ],
})
```

_Orange: the curve you drew. Blue (when $\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}} > 0$):
the realised noisy path actually fed into the integrals. Change the PRNG
seed (either slider — they're synchronised) to cycle through different
noise realisations._

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="phase-c-derived">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">min / max drawn S</div><div class="value">${customScenario.minS.toFixed(4)} / ${customScenario.maxS.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">mean drawn S (I_T / T)</div><div class="value">${customScenario.meanS.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">terminal S_T</div><div class="value">${customScenario.ST.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">tail integral ∫_{τT}^{T} S_t dt</div><div class="value">${customScenario.tailInt.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customRows = [
  { book: "fee",                      pnl: customScenario.feeRev, mcMean: run.fee.mean,       diff: customScenario.feeRev - run.fee.mean },
  { book: "principal (custom)",       pnl: customScenario.cust,   mcMean: run.principal.mean, diff: customScenario.cust   - run.principal.mean },
  { book: "principal (back-to-back)", pnl: customScenario.b2b,    mcMean: run.b2b.mean,       diff: customScenario.b2b    - run.b2b.mean },
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(customRows, {
  columns: ["book", "pnl", "mcMean", "diff"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    pnl: "P&L on drawn curve",
    mcMean: "E[Π] (MC)",
    diff: "drawn − MC mean",
  },
  format: {
    pnl: x => x.toFixed(2),
    mcMean: x => x.toFixed(2),
    diff: x => (x >= 0 ? "+" : "") + x.toFixed(2),
  },
})
```

_The **drawn** column is the deterministic P&L implied by the trajectory
you sketched; the **MC mean** column is $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$ from the Monte
Carlo above; the **diff** shows how this specific scenario compares to
the average across random GBM futures._

::::

## Notes

- **Performance.** Each slider change re-runs the simulator. At 5,000
  paths × 100 steps the wall time reported above should be well under a
  second on a typical laptop. Increase `Monte Carlo paths` for tighter
  tails; decrease for responsiveness while exploring.
- **Reproducibility.** The PRNG is the same seeded Mulberry32 as Phase B,
  so a given `(seed, inputs)` pair is deterministic across reloads.
- **What is still Phase B under the hood.** GBM dynamics and deterministic
  retirement flow. Phase C iterations will replace these with
  jump-diffusion, regime switching, and Poisson demand while keeping the
  same UI and book definitions.
- **What is new beyond Phase B.** The principal book is parametrised by
  the raw inventory state (tokens + cost basis), not by a ratio $\alpha$.
  This allows seeding with inventory bought previously at a price different
  from $S_0$ — a prerequisite for the calibration work later in Phase C.
